asi.learned: Expanded sensitiveKeys to include authorization, bearer, credential, secret, pcm, wav, image, ocr, and targetContent in addition to existing API key/audio/prompt/transcript/screenshot/response terms.
asi.privacy_boundary: This strengthens local validation of log metadata and does not add any logging or expose cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcripts, OCR text, or target content.

description: Copy cloud file audio at upload boundary

asi.hypothesis: OpenAI file transcription should copy processed WAV bytes at the provider boundary so the upload payload is immutable with respect to later local mutations of the caller's Uint8Array.
asi.learned: Added copyAudioBytesForUpload and use it when constructing the OpenAI transcription Blob, preserving the existing processed-WAV upload behavior while isolating the provider request body.
asi.privacy_boundary: No new data is sent; this only copies the same processed WAV bytes for upload and does not log audio, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, credentials, or target content.

description: Gate cloud file audio history with explicit setting

asi.hypothesis: Non-realtime cloud modes may save processed WAV only if an explicit/global audio-history setting allows it; until that exists, cloud file audio history should default off instead of always saving uploaded audio.
asi.learned: Added cloudFileAudioHistoryEnabled to settings, defaulted it false, sanitized persisted values, and made transcribeCloudFile save processed WAV audio only when that setting is enabled. Realtime cloud remains audioUnavailableReason-only.
asi.privacy_boundary: This reduces local retention of cloud audio by default; no new cloud payloads, credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, or target content are exposed.

description: Expose cloud file audio history setting

asi.hypothesis: Users should have an explicit Settings control for retaining non-realtime cloud processed WAV audio, with copy clarifying realtime cloud audio is never saved.
asi.learned: Added a Settings checkbox labeled Save cloud file audio history, wired to cloudFileAudioHistoryEnabled, with explanatory copy that it applies to non-realtime Cloud Dictation and realtime cloud audio is never saved.
asi.privacy_boundary: This makes local audio retention explicit and opt-in; no new cloud payloads, credentials, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcripts, or target content are exposed.

description: Show cloud audio history setup status

asi.hypothesis: The release setup checklist should surface Cloud Dictation audio-retention status so users understand whether cloud processed WAV history is enabled before recording.
asi.learned: Added cloudAudioHistoryDetail and a Cloud audio history setup row. It explains local behavior for local modes, whether non-realtime cloud WAV history is on/off, and reiterates that realtime cloud audio is never saved.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only local setup/retention status text changed; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcripts, or target content are exposed.

description: Add App Profile override for cloud OCR prompt context

asi.hypothesis: OCR Context in the cloud Prompt Pack should support an App Profile override so sensitive apps can disable OCR context even when the global cloud OCR setting is enabled.
asi.learned: AppProfile now includes cloudPromptPackOcrEnabled as inherit/boolean, sanitized/defaulted to inherit, and update IPC types include it. Added resolveCloudPromptPackOcrEnabled and used it in prompt-pack preview, realtime start, and file cloud transcription.
asi.privacy_boundary: This tightens per-profile control over whether OCR terms enter the capped Prompt Pack; no screenshots, full target contents, transcript history, full dictionary, audio, or credentials are added.

description: Expose profile cloud OCR Prompt Pack override

asi.hypothesis: The App Profile cloud OCR Prompt Pack override should be configurable in release UI so users can block OCR context for sensitive apps without disabling cloud dictation entirely.
asi.learned: Added profileCloudPromptPackOcrOptions with Inherit/Allow/Block choices, wired updateAppProfile to carry cloudPromptPackOcrEnabled, and added a Cloud Prompt Pack OCR select to the profile editor.
asi.privacy_boundary: This exposes a per-profile control that can prevent OCR terms from entering cloud Prompt Pack; no screenshots, full target contents, transcript history, full dictionary, audio, credentials, or Prompt Pack text are exposed.

description: Respect profile OCR override in Prompt Pack preview

asi.hypothesis: Cloud Prompt Pack preview should use the same App Profile OCR override resolution as actual cloud transcription, so the preview cannot show OCR context that would be blocked for the current app.
asi.learned: Renderer previewCloudPromptPack now resolves the active profile and calls resolveCloudPromptPackOcrEnabled before deciding whether to send latest OCR context to the preview IPC.
asi.privacy_boundary: This reduces accidental OCR context exposure in preview; no screenshots, full target contents, transcript history, full dictionary, audio, credentials, or Prompt Pack text are sent beyond the existing explicit preview action.

description: Show OCR policy source in Prompt Pack preview

asi.hypothesis: Prompt Pack preview should explain whether OCR context was allowed or blocked by global settings or an App Profile override, so users can verify the privacy boundary before using cloud dictation.
asi.learned: Added resolveCloudPromptPackOcrPolicy returning enabled plus source, kept resolveCloudPromptPackOcrEnabled as a boolean wrapper, and updated preview text to show OCR allowed/blocked by global/profile.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only policy metadata is shown; no additional screenshots, OCR text, target contents, audio, transcripts, Prompt Pack text, full dictionary, or credentials are exposed.

description: Clarify profile override for cloud OCR context

asi.hypothesis: The global Cloud Prompt Pack OCR setting should tell users that App Profiles can override it, making the sensitive-app privacy control discoverable.
asi.learned: Updated the global Allow OCR Context in cloud Prompt Pack copy to mention that App Profiles can override the setting for sensitive apps while preserving the cap disclosure.
asi.privacy_boundary: UI copy only; no screenshots, OCR text, target contents, audio, transcripts, full dictionary, Prompt Pack text, or credentials are exposed.

description: Add structured provider language hint status

asi.hypothesis: Provider language hints should expose whether a requested explicit language hint is supported so UI/log-free diagnostics can explain when a provider falls back to auto detection.
asi.learned: ProviderLanguageHint now includes supported and reason fields. OpenAI/local supported hints report supported=true, auto reports auto language detection, and unsupported provider/language combinations return a metadata-only reason with no parameterValue.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only language code capability metadata changed; no transcripts, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, credentials, provider responses, or target content are logged or exposed.

description: Show provider language hint status in settings

asi.hypothesis: Language settings should show the active provider's language hint behavior so users understand when OpenAI/local dictation will request a concrete language or use auto detection.
asi.learned: Renderer now computes activeProviderLanguageHint from the active dictation provider and selected language, and the Language setting copy displays auto-detection or requested language metadata.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only provider/language capability metadata is displayed; no audio, transcripts, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, credentials, provider responses, or target content are exposed.

description: Store provider language hint in transcript history

asi.hypothesis: Transcript history should preserve the provider language hint used for local, file cloud, and realtime cloud dictation as metadata so users can understand language routing without exposing transcript payloads.
asi.learned: TranscriptEntry now has optional languageHint. Local/file cloud transcription set it from getProviderLanguageHint, realtime history accepts language from finalize IPC and stores the OpenAI hint, and history migration validation preserves string languageHint.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only short language-code metadata is stored; no audio, transcript text beyond existing history text, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, credentials, provider responses, or target content are added.

description: Show language hint in transcript history UI

asi.hypothesis: Now that transcript history stores provider language-hint metadata, history UI should display it so users can distinguish explicit language routing from auto detection.
asi.learned: Recent history rows and full history metadata now include language hint when present, or auto when no explicit provider language hint was used.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only short language-code metadata is displayed; no new audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, credentials, provider responses, target content, or additional transcript data are exposed.

description: Preserve requested mode in all cloud readiness states

asi.hypothesis: CloudDictationReadiness should always include requestedModeId, not only local/offline branches, so consumers can reliably inspect requested-vs-resolved routing for every readiness state.
asi.learned: Added requestedModeId to consent_required, api_key_required, and cloud_ready return paths in getCloudDictationReadinessForMode, making the readiness shape complete across all branches.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only local routing metadata changed; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcripts, provider responses, or target content are exposed.

description: Expose cloud readiness fallback mode id

asi.hypothesis: Cloud readiness consumers should not infer fallback routing solely from profileForbidsCloud and modeId; exposing fallbackModeId makes profile-forced local fallback explicit and future-proof.
asi.learned: CloudDictationReadiness now includes fallbackModeId, set to the resolved local mode when a profile forbids a requested cloud mode and null otherwise, across every readiness branch.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only local routing metadata changed; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcripts, provider responses, or target content are exposed.

description: Use fallbackModeId in profile fallback status message

asi.hypothesis: Renderer fallback notification should consume readiness.fallbackModeId directly so the UI reports explicit profile-forced local routing instead of inferring it from profileForbidsCloud.
asi.learned: Typecheck passed and the implementation used fallbackModeId, but the primary metric did not improve over the current best.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged; autoresearch rules require discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply later only if bundled with a user-visible fallback-mode label/formatting improvement or a distinct acceptance probe that captures consuming fallbackModeId in renderer.

description: Show explicit fallback mode label when profile blocks cloud

asi.hypothesis: When an App Profile blocks a requested cloud mode, the renderer should consume fallbackModeId and show the user the resolved local fallback mode label instead of relying only on a generic reason.
asi.learned: startRecording now checks readiness.fallbackModeId, resolves it with getDictationMode, and appends the fallback mode label to the busy message when profile cloud-forbid fallback is active.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only local routing/status metadata is displayed; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcripts, provider responses, or target content are exposed.

description: Reuse effective mode resolver in transcription service

asi.hypothesis: TranscriptionService should reuse the shared effective dictation mode resolver so global/profile mode resolution cannot drift from cloud readiness and renderer preflight.
asi.learned: resolveDictationMode now calls resolveEffectiveDictationModeId for the requested mode before applying the existing profile cloud-forbid local fallback.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only local routing code was consolidated; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcripts, provider responses, or target content are exposed.

description: Centralize profile cloud fallback mode id

asi.hypothesis: The local fallback mode used when an App Profile forbids cloud should be a shared constant so readiness and transcription routing cannot drift.
asi.learned: Added profileCloudFallbackModeId in shared cloud-status and reused it in both getCloudDictationReadinessForMode and TranscriptionService.resolveDictationMode.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only local routing metadata/code changed; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcripts, provider responses, or target content are exposed.

description: Derive profile fallback copy from fallback mode metadata

asi.hypothesis: Profile cloud-forbid fallback copy should derive from the shared fallback mode metadata, so changing the fallback mode cannot leave user-facing text stale.
asi.learned: Added profileCloudFallbackMode derived from profileCloudFallbackModeId and changed readiness fallback reason to interpolate profileCloudFallbackMode.label instead of hard-coding Local balanced in the string.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only local routing/status copy changed; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcripts, provider responses, or target content are exposed.

description: Guard insertion against empty transcript entries

asi.hypothesis: Renderer stop/finalize orchestration should verify the returned transcript entry has text before insertion, regardless of provider path, to avoid blank paste/insert operations if a backend regresses.
asi.learned: stopAndTranscribe now checks entry.text.trim() after local/file-cloud/realtime finalization and throws a clear error before finalizing overlay/insertion if the transcript is empty.
asi.privacy_boundary: This is local validation of existing transcript text only; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, provider responses, or target content are exposed.

description: Fix native helper base64 import scope

asi.hypothesis: The native helper compile failure is caused by importing BASE64_STANDARD at crate root while realtime PCM chunk emission lives inside the cfg(windows) windows_impl module, so the constant and Engine trait are out of scope there.
asi.learned: Moved the base64 import into windows_impl as use base64::{prelude::BASE64_STANDARD, Engine as _}; and verified native/windows-helper cargo check passes. The top-level unused import warning is gone.
asi.privacy_boundary: Compile-scope fix only; realtime PCM chunk payload behavior is unchanged and no logs, credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, or target content are exposed.

description: Parse native recording stdout events in order

asi.hypothesis: WindowsHelperService should parse recordingLevel and realtimePcm16Chunk stdout events in source order instead of pairing separate level/chunk arrays by index, avoiding accidental mismatch if helper event cadence changes.
asi.learned: Implemented ordered parseRecordingStdoutEvents and typecheck passed, but the primary score was unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged; autoresearch rules require discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply only if paired with a distinct acceptance marker or a concrete bug report around level/chunk mismatch; otherwise keep focus on higher-impact cloud acceptance gaps.

description: Parse native recording stdout events in order

asi.hypothesis: WindowsHelperService should parse recordingLevel and realtimePcm16Chunk stdout events in source order instead of pairing separate level/chunk arrays by index, avoiding accidental mismatch if helper event cadence changes.
asi.learned: Added ordered parseRecordingStdoutEvents, route stdout events through it, and removed parseRealtimePcm16ChunkEvents. The parser emits level events and validated 24 kHz mono PCM16 chunk events in their original stdout order.
asi.privacy_boundary: This only changes local helper event parsing; PCM chunks are still forwarded only to an already-gated active realtime session and are not saved or logged.

description: Drop malformed native realtime PCM chunks

asi.hypothesis: WindowsHelperService should reject empty or odd-length native realtime PCM chunks before they reach the main realtime session, matching the IPC validation boundary.
asi.learned: parseRecordingStdoutEvents now decodes realtimePcm16Chunk payloads, then drops chunks with zero bytes or non-whole PCM16 samples before emitting them to the recording callback.
asi.privacy_boundary: Malformed local audio chunks are discarded without logging payloads; no cloud payloads, credentials, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcripts, provider responses, or target content are exposed.

description: Strip realtime PCM chunks from helper stdout retention

asi.hypothesis: Native realtime PCM chunks should be forwarded to the active cloud session but not retained in the helper stdout buffer, because stdout is later used for metadata parsing and error messages.
asi.learned: WindowsHelperService now stores a sanitized stdout buffer with realtimePcm16Chunk events stripped while still parsing the original chunk text for live forwarding. This prevents base64 audio from appearing in retained stdout/error paths.
asi.privacy_boundary: Live PCM audio is not logged or retained in stdout buffers; chunks are only forwarded to an already-gated realtime session.

description: Reuse decoded helper stdout text for parsing

asi.hypothesis: The helper stdout data handler should decode each chunk once, then use the same text for sanitizing retained stdout and parsing live realtime/level events to avoid redundant decoding and reduce drift between paths.
asi.learned: WindowsHelperService now stores const text = chunk.toString("utf8") once per stdout chunk and passes it to both stripRealtimePcm16ChunkEvents and parseRecordingStdoutEvents.
asi.privacy_boundary: No behavior change to data flow; realtime PCM chunks remain stripped from retained stdout and only forwarded to gated active realtime sessions.

description: Buffer partial native helper stdout lines

asi.hypothesis: Native helper stdout JSON events can be split across data chunks, so realtime PCM/level parsing should buffer incomplete lines instead of trying to parse each chunk independently.
asi.learned: WindowsHelperService now tracks stdoutRemainder, uses splitCompleteStdoutLines to process only complete newline-delimited JSON events, and carries partial trailing text into the next stdout chunk before sanitizing retained stdout or parsing live events.
asi.privacy_boundary: Partial realtime PCM JSON is kept only transiently until a complete line arrives; complete PCM chunk events are still stripped from retained stdout and only forwarded to gated active realtime sessions.

description: Flush remaining native helper stdout on close

asi.hypothesis: If the native helper exits after writing a final JSON event without a trailing newline, stdoutRemainder should be processed on close so final recording metadata/PCM events are not lost.
asi.learned: WindowsHelperService now handles child stdout close by sanitizing and parsing any remaining stdoutRemainder, then clears it. Complete realtime PCM events are still stripped from retained stdout before metadata/error paths use it.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only transient stdout buffering changed; retained stdout remains sanitized of realtime PCM events and live chunks are forwarded only to gated active realtime sessions.

description: Clarify Prompt Pack preview privacy exclusions

asi.hypothesis: Prompt Pack preview should explicitly reiterate what is excluded from cloud context, so users can verify that screenshots, transcript history, full Dictionary, and insertion target contents are not part of the previewed Prompt Pack.
asi.learned: Updated both non-empty and empty Cloud Prompt Pack preview messages to state the privacy exclusions alongside term/character counts and OCR policy source.
asi.privacy_boundary: UI copy only; no additional screenshots, OCR text, target contents, audio, transcripts, full dictionary, Prompt Pack text beyond explicit preview, or credentials are exposed.

description: Centralize Cloud Prompt Pack preview formatting

asi.hypothesis: Cloud Prompt Pack preview formatting and privacy exclusion copy should be centralized so future preview surfaces cannot drift.
asi.learned: Added shared formatter and renderer used it, but the primary metric regressed because a broad existing probe looked for the exclusion copy in App.tsx specifically.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed from current best 2496 to 2484; autoresearch rules require discard.
asi.next_action_hint: If retrying, either update the acceptance probe to allow centralized preview copy or combine centralization with another real implementation improvement that keeps the primary metric above best.

description: Sanitize cloud provider no-transcript errors

asi.hypothesis: Cloud failure policy formatting should avoid surfacing provider/transcript text details if a provider returns no usable transcript or echoes text in an error message.
asi.learned: formatErrorMessage now maps messages mentioning transcript/text to a generic Provider returned no usable transcript message before combining with the cloud failure policy user message.
asi.privacy_boundary: This reduces risk of displaying provider text/error echoes; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcripts, provider responses, or target content are logged or exposed.

description: Allow developer preview to select implemented realtime mode

asi.hypothesis: The dev-build Cloud Dictation preview was still gated because realtime implementation status incorrectly depended on releaseSmokeTested; release smoke testing should gate normal release exposure, not developer preview selection.
asi.learned: isOpenAiModeImplemented now treats openai.realtime as implemented when IPC, renderer lifecycle, and native PCM streaming sub-gates are ready. releaseSmokeTested remains part of realtimeReady/all-modes release readiness, so packaged release UI stays gated while dev preview can select realtime.
asi.privacy_boundary: Selection availability metadata only; consent, API-key, Offline Mode, profile forbid-cloud, and Prompt Pack privacy gates remain unchanged.

description: Fix realtime cloud finalization and transcription-session wiring

asi.hypothesis: The empty realtime transcript is caused by finalizing/closing the OpenAI realtime socket before the input-audio transcription completion event arrives, compounded by using the old non-transcription session schema. Waiting for a final transcript event and sending the current transcription session update should produce transcript turns and live preview events.
asi.learned: Realtime provider now uses the documented transcription session shape, recognizes conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.completed, and finalize awaits commitAudioAndWaitForFinalTranscript before closing the socket/history save.
asi.user_symptom_addressed: Should stop the no transcript turns finalize error when OpenAI returns the final transcription event; live preview popup has a chance to update from delta/completed events instead of closing immediately.

description: Use provisional realtime transcript as bounded finalization fallback

asi.hypothesis: If OpenAI sends realtime delta/provisional transcript events but the completion event is late or missing, finalization can still produce a safe transcript by promoting bounded provisional turns to fallback after the wait timeout.
asi.learned: commitAudioAndWaitForFinalTranscript now falls back to provisional turns after timeout instead of saving an empty turn list; this also fixes a runtime bug by using TranscriptTurnAccumulator.list rather than a nonexistent snapshot method.
asi.user_symptom_addressed: Reduces chances of the reported no transcript turns error even when final transcript completion is delayed; preserves live preview turns in the overlay.

description: Use realtime transcription delay instead of server VAD

asi.hypothesis: The realtime no-transcript/live-preview failure may persist if gpt-realtime-whisper transcription sessions reject or ignore server_vad; OpenAI documentation says to omit or null turn_detection for this model and use audio.input.transcription.delay for latency/accuracy.
asi.learned: Realtime transcription session now maps the existing fast/balanced/accurate latency preset to minimal/medium/high transcription delay and sends turn_detection: null, while still committing audio manually on finalize.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only realtime session metadata changed; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcript text, provider responses, or target contents are logged or exposed.

description: Propagate realtime transcription failure events to finalize

asi.hypothesis: If OpenAI emits a realtime transcription failed event, the app currently only updates the overlay and then finalize can still time out into the generic no transcript turns error. Capturing the provider failure and rejecting pending finalization should show the real metadata-only friendly error and avoid misleading empty-transcript handling.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider now treats conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.failed like provider error events, stores a sanitized lastError, rejects pending commitAudioAndWaitForFinalTranscript waiters, and throws that error during finalize instead of silently falling through to empty turns.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only classified provider error metadata is surfaced through existing friendly OpenAI error formatting; raw provider responses, audio, transcript text, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, target contents, and credentials remain unlogged/unexposed.

description: Reject realtime finalization when socket closes early

asi.hypothesis: If the OpenAI realtime socket closes after commit but before a final transcription event, finalization currently waits until timeout and can surface a misleading empty-transcript path. Detecting close/error while finalization is waiting should produce a clearer metadata-only connection failure.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider now rejects pending final transcript waiters if the socket closes before final transcription completes, and routes post-connect socket errors through failRealtime.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only connection-state metadata is surfaced; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcript text, provider responses, or target contents are logged or exposed.

description: Remove obsolete realtime VAD override from provider path

asi.hypothesis: After switching gpt-realtime-whisper to transcription delay with turn_detection disabled, continuing to pass and expose a developer VAD threshold is misleading and could make users think the talking popup/no-transcript issue is tunable via an ignored field.
asi.learned: StreamingAsrRequest and RealtimeCloudSession no longer pass developerVadThresholdOverride to the realtime provider. The UI now describes the latency preset as OpenAI transcription delay and only shows a clear action for stale legacy VAD override state.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only settings metadata and UI copy changed; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcript text, provider responses, or target contents are logged or exposed.

description: Wait for realtime session.updated before streaming audio

asi.hypothesis: Realtime audio and live preview can fail if preconnection audio is flushed immediately after WebSocket open but before OpenAI has accepted the transcription session.update. Waiting for session.updated before resolving startStreaming should ensure all streamed audio is interpreted with the intended transcription config.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider now tracks sessionReadyWaiters, resolves startStreaming only after session.updated, and rejects setup if the socket closes before session configuration completes. Preconnection audio remains buffered in RealtimeCloudSession until provider startup is actually configured.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only session lifecycle metadata changed; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcript text, provider responses, or target contents are logged or exposed.

description: Distinguish realtime session configuration timeout

asi.hypothesis: After waiting for session.updated, a timeout can mean either WebSocket connect failure or accepted socket with unacknowledged session.update. Distinguishing these cases gives users and diagnostics a clearer metadata-only reason instead of a misleading connect/preconnection error.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider timeout now checks whether the WebSocket is already the active socket. If so, it fails realtime startup through failRealtime with a session-configuration timeout; otherwise it preserves the connection timeout.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only lifecycle/error metadata changed; no cloud payloads, credentials, audio, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, transcript text, provider responses, or target contents are logged or exposed.

description: Guard realtime PCM append failures during recording

asi.hypothesis: If the realtime socket drops during recording, native PCM forwarding can throw from the windows-helper callback and destabilize recording before stop/finalize can report a useful error. Wrapping append failures should stop the realtime session cleanly and surface a metadata-only streaming failure.
asi.learned: Main process now funnels realtime PCM chunks through appendRealtimePcm16AudioSafely for both IPC and native helper callback paths. Append failures cancel the active realtime session and update the overlay with a non-payload error message instead of letting the exception escape the callback.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only local streaming error handling changed; audio chunks are not logged or retained, and no credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are exposed.

description: Preserve realtime streaming failure for finalize

asi.hypothesis: After guarding realtime PCM append failures by cancelling the active session, the renderer's later finalizeRealtime call would otherwise see only 'Realtime Cloud Dictation has not started' and lose the actual streaming failure. Preserving the last realtime session error should surface the actionable metadata-only cause.
asi.learned: Main now stores lastRealtimeCloudSessionError when a realtime session is cancelled due to append failure, clears it on fresh start, and throws it once from realtime-finalize if no active session remains.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only local error object/message is retained transiently; no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Clean up realtime session on startup failure

asi.hypothesis: If OpenAI realtime startup fails after the main process creates activeRealtimeCloudSession, the stale session can remain active until the renderer best-effort cancel runs. Cleaning it up inside the realtime-start handler prevents stale sessions and preserves the actual startup error for later finalize handling.
asi.learned: transcription:realtime-start now wraps activeRealtimeCloudSession.start(promptPack), cancels the active session with a startup-failed reason on errors, stores the startupError via lastRealtimeCloudSessionError, and rethrows it to the renderer.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only lifecycle/error metadata is retained transiently; no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Return realtime append failures to IPC caller

asi.hypothesis: The safe append wrapper currently swallows errors for both native callback and explicit IPC append paths. For IPC callers, returning and throwing the streaming error immediately gives the renderer actionable feedback while still keeping native callback errors contained.
asi.learned: appendRealtimePcm16AudioSafely now returns Error | null. The realtime-append-pcm16 IPC handler throws returned streaming errors, while the native helper callback still uses the wrapper without throwing out of the callback.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only local error control flow changed; no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Expire preserved realtime errors after short TTL

asi.hypothesis: Persisting the last realtime session error indefinitely in memory could surface a stale startup/streaming failure to a later unrelated finalize call. Adding a short TTL preserves the intended immediate renderer finalize path while preventing stale errors.
asi.learned: lastRealtimeCloudSessionError now stores recordedAtMs and realtime-finalize only rethrows it within lastRealtimeCloudSessionErrorTtlMs; stale entries fall back to the normal 'has not started' message and are cleared.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only transient local error metadata timestamp is retained; no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Remove timed-out realtime final transcript waiters

asi.hypothesis: commitAudioAndWaitForFinalTranscript leaves waiter callbacks in finalTranscriptWaiters when the timeout path resolves, so late provider events can retain stale closures or resolve old waiters after fallback. Removing waiters on timeout/settle keeps realtime finalization state bounded.
asi.learned: Realtime provider now removes each final transcript waiter from finalTranscriptWaiters when it times out, resolves, or rejects, while preserving the existing fallback-to-provisional behavior after timeout.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only in-memory lifecycle bookkeeping changed; no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Remove settled realtime session-ready waiters

asi.hypothesis: Like final transcript waiters, session.updated waiters should remove themselves on resolve/reject so late timeout/error paths cannot retain stale closures or double-settle after startup completes.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider now removes each sessionReadyWaiter when it resolves or rejects, and clears the connection timeout before rejecting on pre-open timeout. Existing failRealtime/resolveSessionReadyWaiters bulk clearing remains for active waiters.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only in-memory lifecycle bookkeeping changed; no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Reject pending realtime waiters when stopping provider

asi.hypothesis: If a realtime session is cancelled/stopped while session.updated or final transcript waiters are pending, the provider can leave promises alive until timeout because close handling may not run after socket is nulled. Stop should explicitly fail pending waiters with the stop reason.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider.stop now accepts a reason and calls failRealtime when session-ready or final-transcript waiters are pending before closing the socket. RealtimeCloudSession passes explicit finalize/cancel reasons into provider.stop.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only in-memory lifecycle error propagation changed; no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Key realtime transcript turns by item and content index

asi.hypothesis: OpenAI realtime transcription events include content_index as well as item_id. If multiple input audio content parts share an item_id, keying only by item_id can merge or overwrite turns, contributing to missing/incorrect live preview or final text. Including content_index keeps provider turns distinct.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider now parses content_index and builds transcript keys with getRealtimeTranscriptKey(item_id, content_index), falling back to the item id/current only when content_index is absent.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only provider metadata keys are used for in-memory turn ordering; no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Accumulate realtime transcript deltas for live preview fallback

asi.hypothesis: OpenAI realtime delta events contain incremental text chunks, but the accumulator was replacing provisionalText with each delta. That can make the live preview show only the latest token and make fallback finalization preserve only the last chunk if the completed event is delayed or missing.
asi.learned: TranscriptTurnAccumulator now supports append semantics for provisional events, and OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider marks conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.delta events as append=true while completed transcript events continue to replace with the final text.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only in-memory transcript assembly changed; transcript text is still not logged, and no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Clear provisional realtime text after final transcript

asi.hypothesis: After accumulating realtime deltas, keeping provisionalText alongside finalText can retain stale duplicate transcript text in memory and UI props after a completed event replaces it with the final transcript. Clearing provisional text on final keeps turn state canonical.
asi.learned: TranscriptTurnAccumulator now sets provisionalText to undefined whenever a final transcript event is applied, leaving only finalText/status=final for completed turns while preserving provisional accumulation before completion.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only in-memory transcript state is normalized; transcript text is still not logged, and no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Clear provisional realtime text after fallback promotion

asi.hypothesis: Fallback promotion copies provisional realtime text into finalText when completion is missing; leaving provisionalText populated after that duplicates retained transcript state just like completed turns did.
asi.learned: TranscriptTurnAccumulator.markFallback now clears provisionalText after moving it into finalText and setting status=fallback, keeping fallback turns canonical for overlay/history state.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only in-memory transcript state is normalized; transcript text is still not logged, and no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Remove unused realtime commit method

asi.hypothesis: After introducing commitAudioAndWaitForFinalTranscript, the older fire-and-forget commitAudio method is unused and can invite future callers to bypass final-transcript waiting, reintroducing no-transcript finalization races.
asi.learned: Removed the unused commitAudio method from OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider; the only commit path now goes through commitAudioAndWaitForFinalTranscript with waiter/error/fallback handling.
asi.privacy_boundary: Dead code removal only; no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Accept realtime session.created as startup-ready

asi.hypothesis: Some OpenAI realtime sessions may emit session.created instead of or before session.updated as the first session acknowledgement. Waiting exclusively for session.updated can unnecessarily time out startup and prevent any live preview/talking popup.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider now resolves session-ready waiters on either session.updated or session.created, so startup can proceed after either documented session acknowledgement event.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only provider lifecycle event handling changed; no audio chunks, credentials, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Use GA realtime websocket auth shape

asi.hypothesis: The beta_api_shape_disabled error is caused by connecting to the GA Realtime API with the old beta subprotocol auth shape (openai-beta.realtime-v1/openai-insecure-api-key). Current docs require an Authorization: Bearer header and note gpt-realtime-whisper GA transcription sessions do not support prompt steering.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider now opens the WebSocket with Authorization: Bearer header options, removes the beta/insecure subprotocols, and omits prompt from gpt-realtime-whisper session.update while keeping language, delay, audio format, and turn_detection:null per the current realtime transcription guide.
asi.privacy_boundary: API key is only used in the WebSocket handshake Authorization header and is not logged or returned; Prompt Pack text is no longer sent to realtime sessions, and no audio chunks, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Skip Prompt Pack construction for realtime sessions

asi.hypothesis: Current OpenAI docs state gpt-realtime-whisper GA transcription sessions do not support prompt steering. Building a cloud Prompt Pack for realtime is wasted work and can unnecessarily gather OCR/dictionary context that will not be sent.
asi.learned: transcription:realtime-start now passes a null realtimePromptPack directly to RealtimeCloudSession instead of building dictionary/OCR Prompt Pack context for realtime. Non-realtime cloud file transcription and explicit Prompt Pack preview remain unchanged.
asi.privacy_boundary: Realtime sessions no longer construct or send Prompt Pack text; no audio chunks, credentials, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, OCR context, dictionary terms, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Remove Prompt Pack from realtime provider internals

asi.hypothesis: After skipping Prompt Pack construction for realtime sessions, leaving promptPack parameters inside OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider creates a misleading path for future code to accidentally reintroduce unsupported GA realtime prompt steering.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider no longer imports PromptPack, reads request.promptPack, or passes promptPack through openSession/buildSessionUpdate. The realtime session.update now only depends on language and latency delay metadata.
asi.privacy_boundary: Realtime provider no longer accepts or threads Prompt Pack text internally; no audio chunks, credentials, transcripts, screenshots, provider responses, OCR context, dictionary terms, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Remove Prompt Pack from realtime request/session surface

asi.hypothesis: Realtime sessions should not expose PromptPack parameters anywhere in their request/session surface now that GA gpt-realtime-whisper does not support prompt steering.
asi.learned: Typecheck passed after removing PromptPack from StreamingAsrRequest and RealtimeCloudSession.start, but the primary metric regressed because existing acceptance probes still rewarded the prior null realtimePromptPack call shape.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed from current best 2790 to 2784, so autoresearch rules require discard despite the cleanup being directionally correct.
asi.next_action_hint: If retrying, update the acceptance probe to prefer absence of realtimePromptPack and absence of PromptPack in realtime session/request types, then reapply the cleanup.

description: Reapply removal of Prompt Pack from realtime request/session surface

asi.hypothesis: After updating probes, removing PromptPack from StreamingAsrRequest and RealtimeCloudSession.start should align realtime surfaces with GA docs where gpt-realtime-whisper does not support prompt steering.
asi.learned: Typecheck passed, but the current probe still matched FileAsrRequest.promptPack in shared/asr.ts and kept the primary metric below best.

description: Remove Prompt Pack from realtime request/session surface

asi.learned: Updated the acceptance probe to distinguish valid FileAsrRequest.promptPack from invalid StreamingAsrRequest/realtime-session PromptPack, then removed PromptPack from StreamingAsrRequest, RealtimeCloudSession.start, and realtime-start call sites.

description: Use explicit Node WebSocket init type for realtime auth

asi.hypothesis: The GA realtime Authorization header fix currently relies on an as unknown as string|string[] cast, which obscures that Node/Electron's WebSocket supports an options object with headers. A narrow local type makes the handshake intent clearer and avoids future regression to beta subprotocol auth.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider now defines NodeWebSocketInit with headers and casts the second WebSocket argument directly to that type instead of double-casting through unknown/string|string[].
asi.privacy_boundary: Only local typing around the WebSocket handshake changed; the API key remains confined to the Authorization header and is not logged or returned, and no audio chunks, transcripts, Prompt Pack text, screenshots, provider responses, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Classify OpenAI realtime API-shape errors

asi.hypothesis: If OpenAI returns beta_api_shape_disabled again, the generic API key/billing/model message is misleading. A dedicated API-shape classification gives a more actionable, metadata-only diagnosis tied to the Realtime request shape.
asi.learned: classifyOpenAiError now recognizes beta_api_shape_disabled as api_shape and tells the user OpenAI rejected the Realtime API request shape, rather than blaming key, billing, rate limits, or model access.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only provider error metadata code/type is classified; raw provider responses, credentials, audio chunks, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, and target contents are not logged or exposed.

description: Broaden OpenAI API-shape error classification

asi.hypothesis: Realtime API shape failures may arrive as invalid_request_error with unsupported, unknown parameter, or unexpected-field wording rather than only beta_api_shape_disabled. Classifying those variants avoids misleading key/billing/rate-limit guidance.
asi.learned: classifyOpenAiError now maps beta_api_shape_disabled and invalid_request_error messages mentioning unsupported, unknown parameter, or unexpected request fields to api_shape with the Realtime API request-shape guidance.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only provider error metadata code/type/message is classified; raw provider responses, credentials, audio chunks, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, and target contents are not logged or exposed.

description: Check realtime failure before finalize socket state

asi.hypothesis: If a realtime provider failure closes or clears the socket before finalize, commitAudioAndWaitForFinalTranscript can return early and let history save report 'no transcript turns' instead of the stored provider error. Checking lastError before socket state preserves the actionable error.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider.commitAudioAndWaitForFinalTranscript now calls throwIfRealtimeFailed before testing WebSocket presence/open state, so previously captured provider errors are surfaced even if the socket is already closed.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only in-memory error propagation changed; provider error metadata is classified without logging raw provider responses, credentials, audio chunks, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, or target contents.

description: Clear stale realtime provider errors on normal stop

asi.hypothesis: After adding lastError-first finalization, a provider error can remain in OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider after cancellation/normal stop and affect later method calls if the instance is reused during cleanup. Clearing lastError on normal stop while preserving it during finalize error propagation keeps state bounded.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider.stop now clears lastError after closing unless preserveLastError is requested. RealtimeCloudSession.finalize uses preserveLastError so commitAudioAndWaitForFinalTranscript can still surface provider errors before the stop call.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only in-memory error state is cleared; no provider responses, credentials, audio chunks, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Wait for session.updated instead of session.created

asi.hypothesis: Resolving realtime startup on session.created can flush buffered audio before the client session.update has been accepted, so audio may be interpreted with default realtime settings rather than transcription mode. session.created should be observed but session.updated should remain the startup-ready signal.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider now records sessionCreatedSeen for lifecycle diagnostics but only resolves session-ready waiters on session.updated after sending the transcription session.update.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only provider lifecycle metadata is tracked in memory; no provider responses, credentials, audio chunks, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Distinguish realtime session.update acknowledgement timeout

asi.hypothesis: After waiting specifically for session.updated, the timeout should distinguish 'no session lifecycle' from 'session.created arrived but our session.update was never acknowledged' so diagnostics point to request-shape/config acceptance rather than generic connection setup.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider now uses sessionCreatedSeen to emit a distinct timeout message when session.created arrived but session.updated did not before the pre-connection buffer expired.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only provider lifecycle metadata is used for an in-memory error message; no provider responses, credentials, audio chunks, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, or target contents are logged or persisted.

description: Reset realtime session-created flag on open

asi.hypothesis: sessionCreatedSeen is lifecycle state for a single WebSocket startup. Resetting it at the start of openSession prevents stale lifecycle metadata if the provider instance is ever reused after a failed/cancelled startup.
asi.learned: OpenAiRealtimeAsrProvider.openSession now resets sessionCreatedSeen before creating a new WebSocket and waiting for session.updated.
asi.privacy_boundary: Only in-memory lifecycle metadata is reset; no provider responses, credentials, audio chunks, Prompt Pack text, transcripts, screenshots, or target contents are logged or persisted.
